description:
Goal: make the backend fact boundary explicit before adding code. Signal: docs and ticket text classify old-world backend fields as durable Compiler2 facts, derived emission-local facts, or rejected backend baggage.

Change:
- Update northstar.html and .agent/docs/pipeline.md with the artifact ladder: MaterializedProgram -> AbiReadyProgram -> EmissionReadyProgram.
- Document the accepted durable facts: selected call edges, return delivery, extern marshal classes, effects, callable-boundary obligations, stable emission inventory.
- Document rejected old-world shapes: SpecPlan as a compiler artifact, SpecRegistry/SpecId as semantic identity, AbiFacts native/continuation sets, cont extras, callable wrapper signatures, and formatted telemetry.
- Record that fz-rh2.8.2 resumes only after this epic makes backend consumption mechanical.

Why:
- Without the taxonomy, the next implementation can accidentally make old CPS/codegen details look like compiler facts.

Checks:
- Docs point at current Compiler2 MaterializedProgram/ExecutableNeed facts and describe no behavior that code cannot eventually prove.
- The future implementation tickets are grounded in this taxonomy.
- No code changes are made in this ticket unless needed to keep docs truthful.
title: Compiler2 artifacts: publish ABI and emission fact taxonomy
